message:
fix(engine): harden execution cell lifecycle with proper archival and overflow safety

The CellManager was missing proper archival of finalized cells after
cancellation and session teardown, which could leave stale entries in the
active cell map and cause incorrect state queries. Additionally, session
cells created via ExtensionHostBinding were registered as brand-new
extension cells instead of being inserted as the pre-configured child
cells returned by create_session(), losing their inherited policy and
decision context.

Changes:
- bulkhead: use saturating_mul instead of wrapping multiplication when
  computing pressure threshold to prevent silent overflow on large
  max_concurrent * pressure_threshold_pct products
- cancellation_lifecycle: scope mutable borrow of cell before calling
  archive_cell on the manager, ensuring cancelled cells are moved to the
  closed set with their finalize result
- execution_cell: add insert_cell() to register pre-created cells (e.g.
  session sub-cells) and archive_cell() to move finalized cells to the
  closed set; fix ExtensionHostBinding::create_session to extract the
  child cell from the parent's borrow scope and insert it via the new
  insert_cell method
- extension_host_lifecycle: archive cells after unload cancellation and
  session end, use insert_cell for session creation instead of
  create_extension_cell to preserve inherited cell configuration

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
